fr.elements: Main Building; Open Green Space; Driveway; Landscaped Area; Secondary Building
fr.recommendations: Enhancing the visibility and accessibility of the single access point may facilitate emergency responder entry and efficient navigation. The open terrain surrounding the property could support staging and maneuvering of emergency vehicles, allowing for effective deployment of resources. Establishing clearly marked pathways within the open green space and landscaped area may assist in guiding responders swiftly between the main building and the secondary building. Maintaining clear driveways and ensuring unobstructed movement across open spaces could enhance responder access and operational flexibility, potentially improving overall emergency response outcomes.

wildfire.conditions: Scattered Shrubs; Vegetated Perimeter; Open Terrain; Defensible Space; Scattered Trees; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Open Green Space; Vegetated Access Route; Surrounding Open Space; Limited Side Access
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing the separation between the scattered shrubs and the structure may improve defensible space by reducing fuel continuity near the building. Prioritizing the management of vegetation immediately adjacent to the structure could further reduce potential exposure. Optimizing the existing open green space and surrounding open terrain as fuel breaks can be beneficial in maintaining breaks in fuel continuity. Vegetation management along the access route should focus on preserving both effective clearance and accessibility to support potential firefighting operations. Given the presence of scattered trees and a vegetated perimeter, targeted trimming could facilitate improved defensible space while maintaining healthy vegetation. Addressing the limited side access may involve ensuring the pathways remain clear for firefighter movements, which could enhance suppression support. Regular maintenance of the vegetated perimeter while retaining open areas will contribute to long-term wildfire resilience and property preparedness.
